meta.description: A hyperlocal platform connecting home cooks with neighbors — near PMF with strong evidence, closed learning loops, and a deployed landing page

project.name: LocalEats
project.description: A hyperlocal platform connecting home cooks with neighbors for authentic, home-cooked meals — think Uber Eats meets home cooking.
project.primaryGoal: Expand pilot to 3 additional neighborhoods and prove unit economics at density

mvvStatement.mission: Make home-cooked food accessible to everyone
mvvStatement.vision: Every neighborhood has a food community, not just restaurants
founderAssumptions: Home cooks want to monetize their skills

project.conceptIntake: LocalEats is a platform connecting home cooks with neighbors for fresh, authentic home-cooked meals. Urban professionals are tired of expensive, impersonal delivery food. Home cooks have spare kitchen capacity and want to earn extra income. Think Uber Eats meets home cooking.

personas.name: Urban Foodie Professional
personas.primaryPain: Delivery apps are expensive, impersonal, and the food never tastes as good as home cooking — wants authentic meals from real people
personas.context: Lives in dense urban area, orders delivery 3-5x/week, spends $200+/month on food delivery

personas.name: Home Cook
personas.primaryPain: Loves cooking but can't justify cooking elaborate meals just for one or two people — wants to monetize existing skills and get recognition
personas.context: Enjoys cooking as a creative outlet, has extra kitchen capacity, wants $20-35/hr side income

offers.name: LocalEats Meal Subscription
offers.valueProposition: Fresh, authentic home-cooked meals from your neighbors. 3 meals/week from rotating local cooks. No restaurant markup, no delivery fees.

offers.name: LocalEats Cook Partner Program
offers.valueProposition: Earn $25-35/hr cooking what you already love. Flexible schedule. Food safety training and insurance included.

hypotheses.title: People want alternatives to delivery apps
hypotheses.statement: Urban professionals who order delivery 3+ times per week are dissatisfied with current options and actively seek more authentic, homemade alternatives.

riskRank.rationale: Validated through survey and landing page experiment

hypotheses.title: Home cooks will cook for neighbors
hypotheses.statement: Home cooks in urban areas are willing to cook extra portions for neighbors in exchange for $25-35/hr income, given clear safety guidelines and scheduling flexibility.

riskRank.rationale: Validated through interview series — supply side works

hypotheses.title: Customers pay $49/week for meals
hypotheses.statement: Urban professionals will spend $49/week on 3-4 home-cooked meals delivered from neighbors — verified through pre-sale campaign.

hypotheses.title: Food safety trust can be established
hypotheses.statement: Customers will trust home-cooked food from vetted neighbors when safety certifications, kitchen ratings, and satisfaction guarantees are in place.

riskRank.rationale: Pilot data shows trust signals work — regulatory path still forming

hypotheses.title: Unit economics support growth at density
hypotheses.statement: Platform achieves 30%+ gross margin after covering cook payouts (65%), insurance, and platform costs when neighborhood density reaches 50+ active customers per square mile.

riskRank.rationale: Pilot data shows 26% margin at current density, projected 38% at 3x density — needs validation

evidenceItems.summary: Survey of 234 urban professionals: 71% said they'd try a home-cooked meal delivery service
evidenceItems.rawText: Comprehensive survey across 5 cities. 234 respondents. 71% interested. 42% would order weekly. Top concerns: food safety (58%), variety (22%), price (15%).

evidenceItems.summary: Landing page 8.3% email signup rate over 3 weeks (4,200 visitors) — exceeded 5% threshold
evidenceItems.rawText: Facebook/Instagram campaign in Austin. 4,200 visitors, 349 signups (8.3%). Significantly exceeded 5% threshold. Strong demand signal.

biasFlags.explanation: Single city test — may not generalize nationally

qualityReview.recommendedDisconfirmationTest: Run landing page in 2 additional cities to validate geographic generalizability

evidenceItems.summary: 18 of 25 home cooks willing to cook for neighbors at $25-35/hr
evidenceItems.rawText: Interviewed 25 home cooks in Austin. 18 willing to participate. Requirements: clear safety guidelines (14/18), minimum $25/hr payout (16/18), flexible schedule (all). Strong supply signal.

biasFlags.explanation: Single city, 25 cooks — directional but consistent across demographics

evidenceItems.summary: Pre-sale: 47 customers paid $49 deposit. 82% conversion from interested to paid.
evidenceItems.rawText: 210 waitlist → 172 interested → 47 paid (82% conversion from interested). Strong willingness-to-pay signal at $49/week.

evidenceItems.summary: Pilot delivery data: 91% satisfaction, 68% repeat order rate over 3 weeks
evidenceItems.rawText: 47 customers, 3-week pilot. 91% rated 4/5+. 68% reordered. Average order value: $14.50. Cooks earned avg $32/hr. Strong product-market signals.

biasFlags.explanation: Single neighborhood pilot, 3 weeks — not yet proven at scale

evidenceItems.summary: Pilot unit economics: 26% gross margin, projected 38% at 3x density
evidenceItems.rawText: Revenue \$2,303 (47 customers × \$49). Costs: cooks \$1,378 (60%), insurance \$185 (8%), platform fees \$142 (6%). Net margin: 26%. At 3x density: projected 38% margin from reduced delivery radius.

biasFlags.explanation: Single data point — projections from one pilot may be optimistic

experiments.name: Landing Page Demand Validation
experiments.description: Facebook/Instagram campaign targeting foodies in Austin, TX

experiments.name: Pre-Sale Pricing Validation
experiments.description: Pre-sale campaign with $49 deposit to validate willingness to pay

experiments.name: Neighborhood Density Pilot
experiments.description: Multi-neighborhood pilot to validate unit economics at scale

landingPages.name: LocalEats Austin
landingPages.seoTitle: LocalEats — Home-Cooked Meals from Your Neighbors
landingPages.seoDescription: Fresh, authentic home-cooked meals delivered from neighborhood kitchens

content.headline: Real Home Cooking, Right Next Door
content.subheadline: Fresh meals from your neighbors kitchens — delivered to yours

content.text: Tired of the same 5 delivery options? Expensive, impersonal, and never as good as home cooking.
Weekly meal delivery from vetted home cooks in your neighborhood. $49/week for 3 meals.
"I haven't ordered takeout since joining LocalEats. The food is incredible." — Sarah, Austin

learningLoops.outcomeSummary: Pre-sale campaign: 47 paid deposits at $49/week. Waitlist conversion 82%.
learningLoops.insight: Customers will pay upfront for home-cooked meal delivery when they can see cook profiles and menus in advance. Pricing at $49/week is accepted.
learningLoops.actionTaken: Locked pricing at $49/week. Expanded pre-sale to 2 more neighborhoods.
learningLoops.measurementPlan: Track pre-sale conversion in new neighborhoods. Target: 70%+ conversion from interested to paid.

learningLoops.outcomeSummary: Cook pilot validated: 18 cooks willing at $25-35/hr. Average earnings $32/hr in pilot.
learningLoops.insight: Cook supply is strong at $25+/hr. Safety guidelines are the primary concern, not pay rate. Cooks want flexibility in scheduling.
learningLoops.actionTaken: Designed safety certification process. Built flexible scheduling system.
learningLoops.measurementPlan: Track cook retention and satisfaction scores. Target 80%+ retention after 1 month.

learningLoops.outcomeSummary: Pilot gross margin at 26% is below target 30%. Density is the primary lever.
learningLoops.insight: 26% gross margin is viable but not scalable. The unit economics improve significantly with neighborhood density (projected 38% at 3x). Need to focus marketing on dense areas exclusively.
learningLoops.actionTaken: Focused expansion on neighborhoods with 200+ households within 1 mile. Launched density analysis tool for site selection.
learningLoops.measurementPlan: Track margin per neighborhood cohort. Target 30% margin within 8 weeks of neighborhood launch.

pmfAssessment.explanation: PMF score of 68% indicates Strong Signal. All 4 critical hypotheses are validated with moderate or strong evidence. Evidence distortion is low at 0.08 (only minor bias flags from single-city sampling). The remaining blocker is unit economics at scale (hyp_05), which is currently being tested through the multi-neighborhood density pilot. Recommend preparing for controlled scaling to additional neighborhoods once density pilot confirms margin projections.
